message:
fix: iOS Safari Brotli corruption workaround for spec evolution viewer

Replace fetch() + arrayBuffer() with XHR-based fetchAsArrayBuffer() to
work around iOS Safari corrupting Brotli-compressed arrayBuffer()
responses. Add SQLite magic header validation ("SQLite format 3\0") as
a defense-in-depth check before passing bytes to sql.js.

Add Cloudflare _headers file to serve .sqlite3 files as
application/octet-stream with nosniff, preventing content-type sniffing
that triggers the Brotli decompression path. Set Cache-Control to
public with 24h max-age and disable Accept-Ranges.

Update deploy.sh to cp _headers into dist/ before wrangler deploy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+/index.html
+  Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
+
+/spec_evolution.html
+  Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
+
+/*.sqlite3
+  Content-Type: application/octet-stream
+  Content-Disposition: inline
+  Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400
+  Accept-Ranges: none
+  X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff

@@ -14,5 +14,6 @@ cp og-image.png dist/
 cp twitter-image.png dist/
 cp frankensqlite_illustration.webp dist/
 cp frankensqlite_diagram.webp dist/
+cp _headers dist/
 
 npx wrangler pages deploy dist --project-name frankensqlite-spec-evolution --branch main --commit-dirty=true
